methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $1.3B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $274M - $1.0B.

data.narrative:
Aalyria, a Series B-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Chris Taylor, is valued at $1.3B post-money following $150M+ in total fundraising. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 5 public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers in the sector, including Stoke Space, Hubble Network, and Sift.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x multiplier to the $1.3B last post-money valuation, yielding $2.5B. This reflects typical step-up multiples for Series B companies advancing to later rounds. Peer-median analysis across three same-stage, same-sector comparables (Stoke Space at $1.0B, Hubble Network at $400M, and Sift at $274M) produces a median valuation of $400M, with a range of $274M to $1.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied without disclosed revenue figures.

Triangulating these two methods produces a range of $340M to $2.8B, with a median of $1.4B. The current $1.3B valuation sits near the lower end of this range, closely aligned with the peer-median floor. Key uncertainties include Aalyria's revenue visibility relative to public comparables and the applicability of stage-progression multiples in a volatile aerospace funding environment. The wide dispersion between methods ($400M peer-median versus $2.5B stage-progression) reflects material uncertainty in comparable company selection and appropriate growth multiples for the stage.
